relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy greet the Thessalonian assembly in Father and Lord Jesus.
Grace and peace open the letter.
Their trust grows exceedingly and their love increases.
Their endurance under pressure becomes a testimony among the assemblies.
Endurance is held before Divine justice and the coming reign.
The Divine sees the pressure placed upon them.
Relief is promised to the pressured when Lord Jesus is unveiled.
Apocalyptic justice addresses refusal of Divine knowing and good-news obedience.
Separation from the Lord’s face is named as ruin.
The Lord is glorified in the holy ones and marveled at among those who trusted.
Prayer asks that calling be made worthy and trust-work fulfilled in power.
The name of Lord Jesus is glorified in them, and they in him, by grace.

contemplative_note: 2 Thessalonians 1 is endurance under pressure. The community’s growing trust and love are held inside Divine justice, relief, and grace.
